problem_statement:
[Bug] Resuming from checkpoint causes error on Qwen3-235B
It's not possible to resume from checkpoint after crashing on the new v0.2.0 slime without this error being triggered.

Env:
- 128 H100
- slime v0.2.0.post1
- Qwen3 235B

```
CKPT_ARGS=(
   --hf-checkpoint $SHARED_STORAGE_DATA/hf_models/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507
   --ref-load $SHARED_STORAGE_DATA/megatron_models/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507
   --load $SHARED_STORAGE_DATA/slime-ckpt/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507_slime_v2.0/
   --save $SHARED_STORAGE_DATA/slime-ckpt/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507_slime_v2.0/
   --save-interval 20
)
```

hints_text:
Anyone solved this? I’m hitting the same issue with Qwen3-30A3B.

@zhuzilin @fzyzcjy Any solutions on this? Seems a common issue with the new release.
oh no... really sorry for such bug, we are working on fixing this and adding ci to the save/load ckpt.
iirc I faced issues when using new code to resume from old ckpt (probably b/c the old ckpt misses some tensors), but if that error happens when resuming from new ckpt then it is definitely a bug :/
all_hints_text:
Anyone solved this? I’m hitting the same issue with Qwen3-30A3B.

@zhuzilin @fzyzcjy Any solutions on this? Seems a common issue with the new release.
oh no... really sorry for such bug, we are working on fixing this and adding ci to the save/load ckpt.
iirc I faced issues when using new code to resume from old ckpt (probably b/c the old ckpt misses some tensors), but if that error happens when resuming from new ckpt then it is definitely a bug :/
It seems like an issue with megatron. I just added an ad-hoc patch to megatron, could you give the latest image a try?
> It seems like an issue with megatron. I just added an ad-hoc patch to megatron, could you give the latest image a try?

Thanks a lot for your help! Should I re-train and save a new ckpt? or just use the previously saved ckpts with the latest code?

> It seems like an issue with megatron. I just added an ad-hoc patch to megatron, could you give the latest image a try?

Is the latest docker image compatible with an earlier slime with than main branch like the v0.2.0.post1 release?
